title: GC pause and optimization
key_conclusions: GC pause must be separated from total GC time and from user-visible jank; Perfetto is needed to align GC slices with Main Thread and RenderThread.
A long paused field points toward STW, suspension, root scan, or remark work, while long total time with short paused time points toward concurrent phase cost or scheduler contention.
Low freed bytes is not primarily a pause-optimization problem; it should move the investigation to retained paths, large objects, or non-Java memory pressure.
The trigger reason from Day 12 remains part of the diagnosis because For Alloc, Background, and Explicit GC require different engineering actions.
shallow_points: The article still uses command templates rather than a real Perfetto trace with concrete slices and timestamps.
Exact pause phase names, default collector combinations, and log fields were not validated against a specific Android branch.
The article does not include a real device GC log decoded field by field.
discovered_connections: Day 12 trigger timing now feeds directly into Day 13 pause triage: reason determines whether to inspect allocation churn, background overlap, or explicit callers.
Day 11 source-path indexing is reused as a source path plus trace signal table for heap.cc, collector, root visitor, reference processor, and space code.
Day 10 retained-path guidance remains the correct branch when GC runs but freed bytes stay low.
suggest_future_topics: A later article should decode a real GC log line and align it with Perfetto timestamps.
Profiler and MAT topics should reuse the pause-to-evidence matrix and add screenshots or sample trace tables.
Bitmap and large-object articles should reuse the For Alloc plus LOS pressure path from this article.
knowledge_gaps: Need target-branch validation for exact STW phases and collector behavior in ART.
Need a real Perfetto capture that separates GC phase cost from scheduler contention.
Need ROM/version comparison for GC log fields and whether explicit GC is delayed, suppressed, or run immediately.
applied_reflections: Applied Day 12's knowledge gap by adding a Perfetto-oriented sequence diagram and a decision table that separates GC phase cost from scheduler contention.
Applied Day 12's next-run instruction by keeping the engineering entry table pattern: source path, search term, runtime signal, and tool command.
Applied the standing visual-first feedback by using four Mermaid diagrams, compact matrices, and short command blocks instead of long prose.
visible_changes: Added four Mermaid diagrams because of the more-diagrams-less-prose feedback: pause source path, Perfetto alignment sequence, troubleshooting decision flow, and log-field-to-action flow.
Added pause decomposition, source-path-to-trace-signal, optimization, boundary, and engineering-expression tables to replace dense prose.
Added logcat, Perfetto, meminfo, dumpheap, and AOSP rg command blocks so the article visibly strengthens tooling commands and source paths.
next_run_instructions: Update README so Day 13 is marked complete and Day 14 remains pending.
Update progress.json so current_day becomes 14 and last_generated is refreshed.
Day 14 should visibly apply this run's unresolved version boundary by treating generational GC as branch/version-specific rather than universal.
If GitHub Issues still cannot be read, keep recording the gh auth login or GH_TOKEN blocker in reflection, ledger, and automation memory.

issue_feedback.details: gh issue list --repo hello-he/android-memory-expert --state open --limit 50 --json number,title,body,url failed because GitHub CLI is not authenticated and GH_TOKEN is not populated.
